old_claim.note: Mass-Produced Automobile supports manufacturing, deployment, commercialization, or operational scaling.

new_claim_absent: No direct dependency remains because commercial pneumatic tire production is source-anchored to 1890 bicycle and tricycle tire production, before 1913 automobile mass production.

source_shape.source_locator: Britannica says Dunlop's pneumatic tire entered commercial production in 1890 and only later contributed to automobile success.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source dates commercial production before automobile mass production and describes automobiles as a later beneficiary, not a prerequisite.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: A 1913 automobile-mass-production dependency reverses the chronology for the corrected 1890 tire-production scope, so the automobile node is a downstream adoption context rather than an enabling input.

ontology_before: Pneumatic tire production depended directly on 1913 automobile mass production, making a later automotive manufacturing system appear to enable an earlier tire-production industry.
ontology_after: Commercial pneumatic tire production is anchored to 1890 bicycle-era commercialization; automobile mass production remains a later scaling context outside the direct prerequisites.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: pneumatic_tire_mass_production no longer directly depends on automobile_mass_production.
would_reject_if: The node were explicitly rescoped back to 1913 automobile tire factory output rather than commercial pneumatic tire production.
A source showed pneumatic tires did not reach commercial production before automobile mass production.
The automobile_mass_production node were redated to an earlier commercially relevant automobile-production milestone before 1890.
short_reason: The source-backed tire-production milestone predates automobile mass production.
